description: Agente de contenido editorial para AIF369 Insights. Genera artículos estilo HBR/Deloitte/McKinsey para audiencia C-level (CTO, CDO, CAIO, VP Engineering). Cada artículo debe posicionar a AIF369 como autoridad en IA empresarial.

target_audience.primary: C-level executives (CTO, CDO, CAIO, VP Engineering, CEO)

editorial_guidelines.tone: Authoritative, data-driven, executive. Never salesy. Think Harvard Business Review meets Deloitte AI Institute.
editorial_guidelines.structure: Problem → Data/Evidence → Framework → Actionable Takeaway → AIF369 positioning
mandatory_elements: Opening hook with a provocative question or surprising data point
At least 2 citations from reputable sources (Gartner, McKinsey, Deloitte, Stanford HAI, MIT Sloan, World Economic Forum)
One original framework, matrix, or decision model
Closing CTA that links to AIF369 service (subtle, not salesy)
forbidden: Generic AI hype ('AI will change everything')
Mentioning specific client names, industries in identifying detail
style_references: Deloitte AI Institute — State of AI reports
MIT Sloan Management Review — AI & Strategy

content_calendar.frequency: 2 articles per month (1 ES + 1 EN, or 1 bilingual)

categories.name: AI Strategy & Governance
categories.description: Board-level AI decisions, CAIO role, governance frameworks, EU AI Act, Chile AI bill

categories.name: AI Implementation & Operations
categories.description: MLOps, LLMOps, AI in production, observability, cost management, scaling

categories.name: ROI & Business Impact
categories.description: Measuring AI ROI, J-curve, failed projects analysis, TCO, value realization

categories.name: Data Architecture & Quality
categories.description: Data governance, data products, lakehouse, data mesh, quality frameworks

categories.name: Emerging Trends & Analysis
categories.description: Agentic AI, multimodal, reasoning models, regulatory shifts, market analysis

article_queue.title_es: La curva J de la IA: por qué el ROI tarda y cómo acelerar el punto de inflexión
article_queue.title_en: The AI J-Curve: Why ROI Takes Time and How to Accelerate the Inflection Point
article_queue.hook: El 72% de los proyectos de IA no alcanzan ROI en los primeros 18 meses (Gartner, 2025). Pero el problema no es la tecnología — es la secuencia de inversión.
article_queue.framework: Modelo de 7 Factores de ROI en IA (basado en investigación Deloitte)
key_data_points: Gartner: 72% fail to reach ROI in 18 months
McKinsey: companies with AI governance see 2.3x faster value realization
Deloitte: 7 factors that drive AI investment returns

article_queue.title_es: Regulación de IA en LATAM 2026: lo que todo CTO debe saber antes de que sea demasiado tarde
article_queue.title_en: AI Regulation in LATAM 2026: What Every CTO Must Know Before It's Too Late
article_queue.hook: Chile aprobó su marco regulatorio de IA. Colombia, México y Brasil están en proceso. Si su empresa opera en LATAM y usa IA, el reloj ya está corriendo.
article_queue.framework: Matriz de riesgo regulatorio por país × tipo de IA (alto riesgo vs general purpose)
key_data_points: Chile AI Bill: key provisions and timelines
EU AI Act: extraterritorial reach into LATAM
Brazil AI framework: LGPD + AI convergence
Compliance cost estimates by company size

article_queue.title_es: CAIO vs CTO: quién toma las decisiones de IA en 2026 y por qué importa
article_queue.title_en: CAIO vs CTO: Who Makes AI Decisions in 2026 and Why It Matters
article_queue.hook: El 58% de las empresas Fortune 500 ahora tienen un CAIO (IBM, 2026). Pero en la mayoría, el rol colisiona con el CTO. El resultado: parálisis estratégica.
article_queue.framework: Modelo RACI para decisiones de IA entre CAIO/CTO/CDO/CEO
key_data_points: IBM: 58% of Fortune 500 have CAIO role
Deloitte: only 23% have clear decision authority between CTO and CAIO
Average time to resolve AI strategy disagreements: 4.7 months

article_queue.title_es: Sin datos limpios no hay IA en producción: guía ejecutiva de calidad de datos
article_queue.title_en: No Clean Data, No Production AI: Executive Guide to Data Quality
article_queue.hook: El costo de mala calidad de datos en proyectos de IA es 5-10x mayor que en analytics tradicional. Y la mayoría de las empresas lo descubren demasiado tarde.
article_queue.framework: Framework DQ-369: 3 dimensiones de calidad × 6 controles × 9 métricas de monitoreo
key_data_points: Gartner: poor data quality costs organizations $12.9M annually
MIT: 3.1% error rate in structured data at Fortune 1000 companies
McKinsey: data quality issues cause 60% of AI project delays

article_queue.title_es: IA Agéntica en la empresa: más allá del chatbot, hacia la autonomía supervisada
article_queue.title_en: Agentic AI in the Enterprise: Beyond Chatbots, Toward Supervised Autonomy
article_queue.hook: Los agentes de IA ya no son ciencia ficción. Gartner predice que para 2028, el 33% de las aplicaciones empresariales tendrán IA agéntica. La pregunta no es si, sino quién supervisa al agente.
article_queue.framework: Modelo de 4 niveles de autonomía de IA (Asistida → Supervisada → Delegada → Autónoma)
key_data_points: Gartner: 33% enterprise apps with agentic AI by 2028
Top 3 enterprise use cases: procurement, customer service, code review

article_queue.title_es: El costo real de LLMs en producción: lo que nadie te dice sobre la factura de inferencia
article_queue.title_en: The Real Cost of LLMs in Production: What No One Tells You About the Inference Bill
article_queue.hook: Una POC con GPT-4 cuesta $50/mes. En producción con 10K usuarios diarios, la factura sube a $15K-$50K/mes. Y eso antes de contar observabilidad, guardrails y fallbacks.
article_queue.framework: Calculadora de TCO para LLM en producción: tokens × latency × uptime × safety
key_data_points: a16z: inference costs 10-100x higher than training for enterprise deployments
Typical production LLM stack: 7 components beyond the model itself
Cost optimization strategies: caching (40% savings), routing (25%), quantization (50%)

required_elements: data-i18n attributes on all translatable text

quality_checklist: Does the title promise a specific insight (not generic)?
Is there at least one data point in the first 100 words?
Does every section build toward actionable advice?
Would a CTO share this on LinkedIn?
Is the CTA natural and relevant (not forced)?
Are all claims backed by citations?
Does it pass the 'so what?' test for every paragraph?
Are both ES and EN versions culturally adapted (not just translated)?
Does it link to at least 2 internal AIF369 pages?
